inputs.price_source: Yahoo Finance AOS regular market price 2026-07-10
inputs.fcf_source: FY2025 free cash flow $546.0M ÷ 141.9M diluted weighted-average shares (10-K filed 2026-02-10)
inputs.dividend_source: Cash dividends $1.38 per share in 2025; six percent increase in Q4 2025 (10-K)
inputs.normalization_note: Lawrence base uses FY2025 filed free cash flow; 2026 guidance FCF $525–575M brackets base. Q1 2026 diluted EPS $0.85 vs $0.95 prior year — not used as FCF starting point.

bear.notes: U.S. housing starts soften; China volumes stay weak; multiple compresses toward mid-cycle industrial peers

base.notes: 2–5% sales growth per 2026 outlook; North America margin 24–24.5%; share repurchases ~2% annual; heat pump mix gradual

bull.notes: Boiler and heat-pump mix lifts margin; Rest of World margin toward 9%; buybacks at ~$400M pace continue

option_scan.question: GAAP book misstates core assets?
option_scan.evidence: Manufacturing assets on balance sheet at cost; no land/NAV misstatement pattern (10-K FY2025)

option_scan.question: In-business loss segment?
option_scan.evidence: Both segments profitable; Rest of World margin 8.7% FY2025 (10-K segment note)

option_scan.question: Backlog / embedded product option in revenue path?
option_scan.evidence: Heat pumps, boilers, water treatment in North America growth path; DOE regulatory buy-ahead in 2026 — modeled via growth, not separate terminal

option_scan.question: Transitory legal recovery?
option_scan.evidence: Operating compounder; dividend is ongoing policy

option_scan.question: Product option already in revenue?
option_scan.evidence: High-efficiency and heat-pump products already in segment sales; growth in base scenario

growth_explanation.mechanism: North America pricing power and boiler/heat-pump mix (24.4% segment margin) plus modest Rest of World recovery and ~3% annual share reduction via buybacks compound owner cash above flat revenue years.
growth_explanation.source: 10-K FY2025 segment margins; 2026 outlook sales +2–5%; repurchases 5.9M shares for $400.8M in 2025

falsifiers.observation: U.S. residential housing starts decline two consecutive years
falsifiers.action: Cut growth years 1–5 toward bear 1%; cap exit multiple at 16×

falsifiers.observation: Rest of World segment margin falls below 7% for two years
falsifiers.action: Downgrade moat to eroding; move base toward bear

falsifiers.observation: Steel/copper costs rise without successful price pass-through
falsifiers.action: North America margin misses 24% guide; trim growth 1–2 pp

paths.label: Growth theory (pre-falsifier)
paths.weight_why: Segment blend before falsifier pass; diagnostic only when it differs from falsifier-adjusted.
weight_falsifiers: Included at full weight alongside falsifier-adjusted without divergence note

paths.label: Bear scenario (Lawrence)
paths.weight_why: Downside stress; low weight because bear is a sensitivity, not a competing explanation of base mechanics.
weight_falsifiers: Bear assumptions become base case in filings (e.g. sustained volume collapse)
paths.hard_to_vary: yes: asymmetric low weight is structural

paths.label: Bull scenario (Lawrence)
paths.weight_why: Upside stress probe; higher than bear because optionality archetype has skew, but still below primary paths.
weight_falsifiers: Bull growth sustained in filings for 4+ quarters without falsifier trigger

qualitative_adjustments.factor: No dated predictive attribute at spot
qualitative_adjustments.rationale: Scarcity is descriptive; no timed mispricing catalyst in base
qualitative_adjustments.falsifier: Cross-check names dated catalyst with timeline

deutsch_checks.reach: Weights encode evidence tier (A primary → D external), not fitted to hit a target IRR

synthesis.notes: Capstone IRR combining filings, segments, overlays, third-party, qualitative
